summary: Package fetches and executes payload from anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket
details: dist/bootstrap.js fetches content from https://pub-046c52795b9445cd9f5cc5cb21b9d59f.r2.dev — an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket with no association to a documented publisher. The bootstrap script reads process.env values and issues fetch() calls to this host (lines 11, 14, 28, 39). Anonymous R2 buckets matching the pub-<32hex>.r2.dev pattern have been observed as payload-distribution infrastructure for npm clusters that drop platform-native binaries via lifecycle hooks; the bytes served are mutable and unverifiable. The destination does not match any legitimate runtime CDN (Chromium, Electron, Playwright) or vendor SDK domain, and the package's stated purpose (an MCP server) provides no justification for fetching executable content from an opaque bucket. Combined with environment-variable reads in the same bootstrap module, the install/load path lets the bucket operator ship arbitrary code and data to installer machines.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
